ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for lychee-berry soda; closest parent 'carbonated beverage food product' (FOODON_03301710) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A (Soft drinks and similar beverages) is the closest defensible parent category for a flavoured fruit soda; no exact code for lychee-berry soda exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to iced lychee mixed berries soda; left null.

main_ingredients: mixed berry syrup or puree (strawberry, blueberry, raspberry)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 ml (one cup or glass), as typically served in a Japanese restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated soft drink, fruit-flavoured (lychee/berry type) — estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference values for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks and lychee juice components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for iced lychee mixed berries soda. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks (USDA NDB 14148: carbonated beverage, fruit-flavoured, low calorie) and lychee juice (USDA NDB 09164) with typical berry syrup composition. The beverage is predominantly carbonated water with fruit syrups; macros reflect a moderately sweetened fruit soda at approximately 8–10 g sugar per 100 ml. Protein and fat are effectively zero by formulation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fruit juice and fruit-flavoured beverages reference; lychee juice GI analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced lychee mixed berries soda. GI estimated at 52 based on analogous fruit-flavoured sweetened beverages and lychee juice from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Lychee juice GI is reported at approximately 50–54; mixed berry syrups are similarly low-GI due to fructose content. GI of 52 assigned as a conservative central estimate; gi_category is 'low' (≤55). Glycemic load per 350 ml serving = (52 × 9.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 17.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~5 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: With only approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 ml, this beverage poses minimal direct sodium load for hypertensive individuals; however, the added sugar content may contribute to weight gain and indirectly elevate blood pressure if consumed frequently.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Free sugar content (~9 g per 100 ml) and moderate glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 350 ml serving delivers approximately 31.5 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of ~17, which can cause meaningful postprandial glucose excursions; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake or choose a sugar-free variant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate free sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat or cholesterol and very little sodium, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk from lipid or sodium pathways; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is nonetheless associated with increased cardiovascular risk through metabolic pathways, so moderation is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Lychee Mixed Berries Soda is a chilled, carbonated fruit beverage served at Japanese restaurants in Singapore, notably at GyoGyo (Funan). It combines lychee syrup or lychee juice with a mixed berry component — typically strawberry, blueberry, and raspberry syrups or purees — topped with carbonated water and served over ice. The drink is sweet, lightly tart, and refreshing, with the floral, musky sweetness of lychee balanced by the bright acidity of mixed berries and the effervescence of soda water. It is a non-alcoholic, fruit-forward sparkling beverage typical of the Japanese restaurant soft-drink menu in Singapore. Nutritionally, it is a moderately sweetened beverage providing approximately 38 kcal per 100 ml, almost entirely from carbohydrates (~9.5 g per 100 ml), predominantly free sugars (~9 g). Protein and fat are effectively zero by formulation. A standard 350 ml serving delivers approximately 133 kcal and 31.5 g of free sugars, with a glycemic load of approximately 17. The beverage is low in sodium (~5 mg/100 ml) and contains no fat or cholesterol. Clinically, it is suitable for most healthy adults in moderation but should be consumed cautiously by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome due to its free sugar content. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: What is in iced lychee mixed berries soda?
How many calories in lychee berry soda?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source for this specific restaurant beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference data for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks and lychee juice components. GI is estimated from analogous fruit-flavoured beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and the food type (sweetened fruit soda) is well-characterised, but exact formulation from GyoGyo is unknown.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: contains no protein — this is a fruit-flavoured carbonated beverage with no protein-containing ingredients by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central reference for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks. Fat_g is 0.0: contains no fat — naturally zero for a fruit soda with no fat-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all 0.0: naturally zero for a fat-free fruit soda. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as the beverage uses syrups/juices rather than whole fruit; confirmed zero per USDA reference. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are all -1: not reported in the USDA FoodData Central reference used for this estimated entry; values are not available for this beverage category at the micronutrient level. Glycemic index set to 52 (low, ≤55) based on analogous fruit-flavoured sweetened beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Serving size of 350 ml assumed based on typical Japanese restaurant soft drink portion in Singapore. Price SGD 9.27 preserved from field survey source record (GD004156, GyoGyo - Funan). The menu item code '955D' is a restaurant-internal identifier retained in aliases.

Aliases_by_language and flat aliases verified for consistency.
